One Key. Symmetric encryption (or “private key” encryption) is called “private key” because the use of a single encryption key necessitates that the key is always kept private.
Direct Memory Access (DMA) is a technique that allows data to be transferred between memory and input/output devices without involving the processor.

Dial-up: Uses a traditional telephone line to connect to the internet. This method is slow and can be interrupted by phone calls. Broadband: Uses various technologies like cable, fiber optic, or DSL to provide high-speed internet access. It offers significantly faster speeds than dial-up and is not interrupted by phone calls. Examples of Internet Service Providers (Broadband): Jio, Airtel, Vi, BSNL, Atria Convergence Technologies, Hathway, Exitel Broadband.

Auto Sum function. The Average function is typically listed in the AutoSum function drop-down menu in spreadsheet software like Microsoft Excel. Skype is a communication platform that allows users to make voice and video calls, send instant messages, and share files over the internet.

8 characters. In Microsoft Excel, the default width of a column is set to accommodate 8.43 characters, which is approximately 64 pixels. Cell Alignment: Horizontal Alignment- General , Vertical Alignment- Bottom. Cell Fill Color: No fill (white cm) on all sides. Zoom Level: 100%.

Bottom Alignment. This option ensures that the text is positioned at the bottom edge, leaving space above it. Center Alignment: Centers text horizontally in the cell or text box. Justify Alignment: Aligns text evenly along both the left and right margins. Distributed Alignment: Distributes text evenly across the width, with equal spacing between characters and words.

conventional encryption. Public key encryption, also known as public key cryptography. The Hybrid Encryption primitive combines the efficiency of symmetric encryption with the convenience of public key (asymmetric) cryptography. Asymmetric encryption is a security technique that uses two different keys to encrypt and decrypt data.

Range of cells or groups of cells. MS Excel, a worksheet, also known as a spreadsheet, is a table into which you enter data and data label. Worksheet used in Excel documents is a collection of cells organized in rows and columns. It is the working surface you interact with to enter data. superscript. In Microsoft Word, a superscript is a number or text that is printed above the baseline, smaller than the regular text. It is often used to represent exponents, footnotes, or endnotes. Examples: 10̣2 - 2 above 10 is called the superscript.
Page Layout Tab : This is where you adjust the layout settings of your page, including margins, orientation, and size.
Pie Chart: A circular chart divided into sectors, each representing a proportion or percentage of the whole.

docx file is a Microsoft Word document saved in the Open XML format. The .docx file extension is a combination of the words "doc" and "xml" and is an evolution of the older .doc format. Q.1696. Which was the first programmable general purpose computer in the world ? SSC CHSL 04/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) ENIAC (b) Mark I (c) Napier's bones (d) Pascaline Sol.1696.(a) ENIAC. Designed by John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert. Mark I was designed in by Harvard graduate student Howard H. Aiken to solve advanced mathematical physics problems. In Excel, cell alignment is how your text or numbers are positioned in the cell. You can align vertically, meaning towards the top, the middle or the bottom. And also align horizontally, meaning to the left, the center or to the right.

Justified alignment is typically used in paragraph formatting to align text to both the left and right margins. Four types of phishing : Spear Phishing, Whaling, Vishing, Email Phishing. More types of cyber attacks: Malware (short for “malicious software”) is a file or code, typically delivered over a network, that infects, explores, steals or conducts virtually any behavior an attacker wants. Spoofing is the act of disguising a communication from an unknown source as being from a known, trusted source. Q.1737. What protocol is commonly used for sending and receiving emails? SSC CHSL Tier II 10/01/2024 (a) SSH (Secure Shell Protocol) (b) SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) (c) FTP (File Transfer Protocol) (d) HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) Sol.1737.(b) The Secure Shell (SSH) protocol is a method for securely sending commands to a computer over an unsecured network.
Logical operations: comparisons (greater than, less than, equal to), AND, OR, NOT. The primary function of an ALU is to store and retrieve data from memory. There can be more than one ALU in a CPU.

Win + E. File explorer shortcuts in Windows 11: Alt + D: Select the address bar. Ctrl + E: Select the search box. Ctrl + N: Open a new window. Num Lock + asterisk (*): Display all subfolders under the selected folder. Num Lock + plus (+) : Display the contents of the selected folder. Num Lock + minus (-): Collapse the selected folder.

DHCP . It is a network protocol that is used to configure network devices to communicate on an IP network. An Internet Protocol (IP) address is the unique identifying number assigned to every device connected to the internet. IP (version 4) addresses are 32-bit integers that can be expressed in hexadecimal notation. An IPv6 address is 128 bits long compared with the 32 bit address of IPv4.

INDIRECT. Other functions: VLOOKUP - Looks for a value in the leftmost column of a table, and then returns a value in the same row from the column we specify. TRANSPOSE - Converts a vertical range of cells to a horizontal range, or vice versa. MATCH - Returns the relative position of an item in an array that matches a specified value in a specified order.
address bus. A computer bus is a set of conductors that enable information to be transmitted between computer components, such as printers, modems, and monitors.

www.india.in. A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is a unique identifier used to locate a resource on the Internet. http: This is the protocol used for communication, in this case, indicating Hypertext Transfer Protocol.

packet filtering. It is a powerful security technique against intrusions from external networks. An application-level gateway (ALG) is a type of firewall that filters traffic at the application layer of the OSI (open systems interconnection) model. Q.1745. A call to action (CTA), if defined, is an instruction to the reader that encourages them to take immediate action.
Compact. In Windows 11 under View menu (Tiles, content, details pane, preview pane, show). Under Show menu (Navigation pane, compact view, item check boxes, file name extensions, hidden items).

Worm. A boot sector virus is a type of malware that infects a system's boot partition or the Master Boot Record (MBR) of a hard disk. A macro virus is a computer virus written in the same macro language used to create software programs such as Microsoft Excel or Word.
two. When the sender and the receiver of an e-mail are on different systems, we need two UAs (User Agents) and a pair of MTAs (client and server). MTAs- Message transfer agents.

RAM is a computer or laptop’s short-term memory. It’s where the data is stored that the computer processor needs to run applications and open files. The information stored in RAM can be checked with the help of BIOS (Basic Input / Output System). BIOS is the program a computer's microprocessor uses to start the computer system after it is powered on. RAM is volatile in nature, which means the data is lost when the device is switched off.

Cells are the basic building blocks of a worksheet. A worksheet (also known as a spreadsheet) consists of cells in which we can enter and calculate data. The active cell is the selected cell in which data is entered when we begin typing. A range is a group of two or more cells in a worksheet. Q.1751. Which networking device operates at Layer 2 of the OSI model and forwards data based on MAC addresses? SSC CGL Tier II 26/10/2023 (a) Switch (b) Hub (c) Firewall (d) Router Sol.1751.(a) Switch. A network switch connects devices in a network to each other, enabling them to talk by exchanging data packets. A hub is a device that links multiple computers and devices together. A router is a device that connects two or more packet-switched networks.

A tape drive is a device that stores computer data on magnetic tape, especially for backup and archiving purposes. DVD Rewritable (DVD RW) discs are designed to store up to 4.7GB or 120 minutes of DVD-quality video.

Facebook (social networking site). Examples of social networking sites: Twitter, Instagram, Linkedin, Snapchat, Google meet. A web browser is an application for accessing websites and the Internet. Examples: Mozilla Firefox, Google Chrome, Opera, Safari, Microsoft Edge, Netscape Navigator, Mosaic.

Cache memory is a chip - based computer component that makes retrieving data from the computer's memory more efficient. It is an extremely fast memory type that acts as a buffer between RAM and the CPU. It holds frequently requested data and instructions so that they are immediately available to the CPU when needed. It is costlier than main memory or disk memory but more economical than CPU registers.

A firewall is defined as the system which analyzes and filters incoming or outgoing data packets based on predefined rules. Q.1756. In computer registers, MAR stands for ______. SSC CGL Tier II 26/10/2023 (a) Main Access Register (b) Memory Access Register (c) Memory Address Register (d) Main Address Register Sol.1756.(c) MAR (Memory Address Registers). It holds the address of the location to be accessed from memory. MAR and MDR (Memory Data Register) together facilitate the communication of the CPU and the main memory. Memory Data Registers: It contains data to be written into or to be read out from the addressed location. More types of registers : AC (accumulator), DR (Data registers), PC (Program counter), IR (index registers), MBR (Memory buffer registers).
A Trojan virus spreads through legitimate-looking emails and files attached to emails, which are spammed to reach the inboxes of as many people as possible.

File explorer keyboard shortcuts: Num Lock + plus (+): Display the contents of the selected folder. Num Lock + minus (-) : Collapse the selected folder. Ctrl + Shift + E: Display all folders above the selected folder. Ctrl + Shift + N: Create a new folder.

Windows logo key + D. Windows logo key + G - It is used to open Xbox Game Bar when a game is open. Windows logo key + C - It is used to open chat from Microsoft Teams. Windows logo key + H - It is used to Launch voice typing.

Photo. Parts of an email address are :- Username- The username is the name we choose to be identified with for e-mail purposes and that we have provided to the email host to create our email account. '@' symbol- @ (pronounced "at the rate of" ) is the symbol in an Email address that separates the name of the user from the user's Internet address. Mail Server - A mail server transfers and delivers email messages between two or more mail clients. Domain - A domain name forms part of the Uniform Resource Locator (URL) and is the address for a webpage. Example:- ssccglpinnacle@gmail.com.
(i) True (ii) False. In Microsoft Word, templates are pre -designed documents. The template could be for a business card, brochure, resume, presentation.

Black Hat. A white hat (ethical hacker) is an individual who uses hacking skills to identify security vulnerabilities in hardware, software or networks. A red hat is a hacker who takes aggressive steps to stop black hat hackers. A gray hat is a computer security expert who sometimes violates ethical standards but doesn't have malicious intentions.
PS/2 port:- The PS/2 port is a 6-pin mini-DIN connector used for connecting keyboards and mice to a PC compatible computer system. It is also called a mouse port.

120 mm. Blu-ray Disc is 120 mm in diameter and 1.2 mm thick, the same size as DVDs (digital versatile/video disks) and CDs (Compact disks). Currently, 50GB and 25GB capacity, Blu-ray Disc media is available in both the BD-RE rewritable and BD-R recordable formats.

Control. A control unit handles all processor control signals. The control unit is made up of flip-flops, logic gates, digital circuits and encoder and decoder circuits that are wired in a specific and fixed way.
Repeater operates at the physical layer. It is a powerful network hardware device that regenerates an incoming signal from the sender before retransmitting it to the receiver.

Some other shortcut key in MS-Excel 365: Ctrl + 0 - To hide the selected column, Ctrl + 1 - Open the Format Cells dialog box, Ctrl + 2 - Bold text or remove bold formatting, Ctrl + - Italicize text or remove italic formatting, Ctrl + 5 - Apply or remove strikethrough formatting, Ctrl+6 Hides and shows hidden objects, Ctrl + 8 - Display and hide the outline symbols, Ctrl + 9 -To hide a row.

N-1. Network topology - Arrangement of a network, comprising nodes and connecting lines. There are 6 types of topology, Bus Topology - Every computer is connected to a common bus (single cable), Ring Topology - Every node is connected to two other nodes, forming a loop. Tree Topology - A central root node from which all other nodes branch out, Star Topology - Each device on the network is connected to a central hub, Mesh Topology ({n(n-1)/2})- Each node is connected to every other node in the network, Hybrid Topology - It is a combination of two or more network topologies.

Ctrl + Left arrow key. Ctrl + The Shift + left arrow and Shift + right arrow combinations - moving the cursor to the beginning and the end of a line respectively. Q.1770.
